Definition: Base price quoted by supplier per unit
Notes: Currency normalization: use spot rate on quote date, add 3-5% FX buffer for volatile currencies (TRY, ARS, ZAR)

Definition: Cost to move goods from supplier FCA/FOB point to your receiving dock
Notes: For sea freight: include demurrage allowance of 2-4% of FOB value. Red Sea routing adds 10-14 days + ~15% freight premium in 2026

Definition: Customs duties applied at country of entry based on HTS/CN code
Notes: Verify HTS code with customs broker. Section 301 (US/China) or anti-dumping adds significantly for covered categories

Definition: Carbon Border Adjustment Mechanism cost (EU imports of covered categories from 2026)
Notes: Applies to iron/steel, aluminium, cement, fertilisers, electricity, hydrogen from non-EU origin. Estimate €30-50/tonne embedded CO2 in 2026 forecasts

Definition: Cost of holding inventory during extended lead time (capital + storage + obsolescence)
Default Calculation Basis: (Unit price × annual demand × carrying rate × extended lead time in years)
Notes: Carrying rate: 18-25% annual is typical (cost of capital 8% + storage 5% + insurance 2% + obsolescence 5-10% by category)

Definition: Cost of quality failures including rework
Notes: (PPM defect rate / 1M) × rework multiplier × unit price
column7: Rework multiplier rule of thumb: 3× unit price for reworkable defect, 10× for scrap, 30× for field-failure recall

Definition: Cost of capital implication of accepting or offering payment terms
Default Calculation Basis: ((Target terms - Actual terms) / 365) × unit price × cost of capital
Notes: If your target is Net 60 and supplier only accepts Net 30, you lose 30 days × 8% / 365 = 0.66% of unit price effectively

Definition: One-time cost of changing supplier spread over first-year volume
Notes: Typical for custom tooling: €15k-€50k. Audit cost: €3k-€8k. Internal qualification: 40-120 hours × loaded cost

Definition: Implicit cost of no dual-source option (premium paid for concentrated dependency)
Default Calculation Basis: Unit price × (0-5%) depending on single-source exposure
Notes: Quantify by asking: if this supplier failed next quarter, what is your 90-day fallback cost? Price a 3% premium for critical categories with no dual source

Definition: Cost of forward contracts or options to lock FX rate
Default Calculation Basis: Approximately 0.5-1.5% of contract value for 12-month hedge
Notes: For non-EUR quotes over €250k, standard practice is to hedge 50-70% of exposure. Your CFO will have a house view
Compare to the 8.95 unit price: this supplier's true cost is 30% higher than the headline. Common pattern for long-lead-time offshore offers
